title: Karatas makoyana
uses: Karatas makoyana has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region, for its potential medicinal properties. It has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions to alleviate these conditions. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Karatas makoyana for medicinal purposes. More studies are required to fully understand its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Karatas makoyana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Karatas, which is native to tropical regions. Karatas makoyana is a climber, often found in the wild, and is native to Southeast Asia. Further classification of the plant is not well-documented, but it is known to be part of the family of dogbane and oleander. The classification of Karatas makoyana continues to be a subject of research in botany.
